title: Jedi Quest: The Moment of Truth
content:
{{Top|rwm|imp|italics=1}} {{Redlink}} {{Book |image=[[File:The_Moment_of_Truth.jpg]] |title=''Jedi Quest: The Moment of Truth'' |author=[[Jude Watson]] |cover artist=*[[Alicia Buelow]] *[[David Mattingly]] |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |early release= |release date=[[November]] [[2003]] |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Paperback |pages= |isbn=0439339235 |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=[[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' |series=[[Star Wars: Jedi Quest (novel series)|''Star Wars: Jedi Quest'']] |preceded by=[[Jedi Quest: The Shadow Trap|''The Shadow Trap'']] |followed by=[[Jedi Quest: The Changing of the Guard|''The Changing of the Guard'']] }} {{Quote|I haven't felt you this tense since... since we fell into that nest of gundarks.|[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] to [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]|{{Film|II}}}} '''''Jedi Quest: The Moment of Truth''''' is the seventh in a [[Star Wars: Jedi Quest (novel series)|series]] of young reader novels that chronicle the adventures of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] between [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]. ==Publisher's summary==
'''Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker.'''
'''Master and apprentice.'''
'''Chosen by fate. Destined for conflict.'''
''There is increasing tension between Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker. Anakin still feels responsible for the death of a Jedi Council member—a guilt he does not think Obi-Wan can help him overcome.'' ''Obi-Wan feels he is a less-than-perfect Master—trying to teach a less-than-perfect apprentice.'' ''It is with these feelings that Obi-Wan and Anakin head to the last free planets of the Uziel system to make a daring rescue attempt. When the rescue goes awry and Anakin is taken prisoner, the Master and Padawan must overcome their conflict—or a planet will die.'' ==Plot summary== [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin]] are on a [[Mission to Typha-Dor|mission]] to save a group of workers from the surveillance post of [[Typha-Dor]] located on [[TY44]], a moon of Typha-Dor. The workers are holding valuable information about the invasion plans of Vanqor's fleet. [[Vanqor/Legends|Vanqor]] is a world with few resources but they are strong militarily and have conquered all the planets in the [[Uziel system]], except for Typha-Dor. After rescuing the workers, Anakin and Obi-Wan are forced to land on Vanqor because their ship is under attack, crippled, and nearly out of fuel. Anakin and the TY44 crew are then captured and taken to a [[Tomo Camp|prison camp]] amidst the [[Tomo Craters]]. The camp is used as a place for insane galactic scientist [[Jenna Zan Arbor]] to experiment with her new drug, the [[Zone of Self-Containment]], which is tested on Anakin. Obi-Wan rescues Anakin, and (after fighting through a [[Gundark/Legends|gundark]] nest) with the help of other [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], they begin their journey to Typha-Dor—only to learn the invasion has already begun. In the end, Typha-Dor's [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], with the help of the Jedi, is able to get Vanqor to surrender and undergo peace negotiations with [[Van-Ith]], the ruler of Vanqor. ==Continuity== The front cover shows [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] wielding a [[Color|green]] [[lightsaber]], even though [[Anakin Skywalker's first lightsaber/Legends|his lightsaber]] was blue at the time. This book features Anakin and Obi-Wan's confrontation with a nest of [[Gundark/Legends|gundarks]], which was inspired by the dialog at the beginning of ''Attack of the Clones''. ==Media==
